Winter Weather Advisory until 3 pm for Mason and Robertson counties. Snow amounts at 1" or less, but roads are cold & could be slick.

byWKRC

CINCINNATI (WKRC) - A massive tech conference for high school students is happening the weekend of Feb.17.



WKRC



Interalliance is hosting the 15th annual Techolympics at Procter and Gamble tower.

Students are competing in coding, video editing, engineering and more.

Those not looking to compete can still participate in workshops to improve their skills.

Two Mason High School students helped with marketing for this year's event.

"Main thing being able to give opportunities to students in the tech field is really important because it's really hard to find those if you don't have the connections or the people and just the events to show you those opportunities," explained Jajoo Ananya.

"I feel like a lot of my friends are scared to talk about tech or be in to it, so just getting them to come to events like this to that its more it's not as intimidating as it might seem at first is really valuable i think," added Estelle Apewokin.

The Techolympics will wrap up Feb. 18.

2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: Students compete in coding, engineering and more at Techolympics
description: A massive tech conference for high school students is happening the weekend of Feb.17.
